formula_note: Retains the retrospective 659 soldiers + 349 policemen breakdown as the best recoverable military/security direct-death figure for the campaign.
uncertainty_note: This is the weakest core numerator in the bundle because it comes from retrospective commemorative reporting rather than a transparent contemporaneous casualty dataset.

formula_note: Low and high are the published HRW bounds; best is the midpoint.
uncertainty_note: This is a strong campaign-level civilian numerator, but it remains a documented range rather than a single official count.

formula_note: Military exposure scenarios: (85,000+5,000), (100,000+7,000), and (114,000+13,000), multiplied by 78/30.44 months.
uncertainty_note: This is an official-force floor. It excludes the full reserve force and irregulars and almost certainly understates total police/security exposure relative to the military/police death numerator.

formula_note: Civilian stock starts from 11,206,847 population, subtracts the military scenario stock, then subtracts full / half / none of the 862,979 documented expulsions before multiplying by 78/30.44 months.
uncertainty_note: Campaign-wide civilian exposure is supportable, but the displacement adjustment is necessarily crude because no FRY-wide daily stock series was recovered.
quality_note: Reasonable campaign-level denominator with explicit scenario bounds.

formula_note: dr_v1_direct = (mil_deaths_direct / mil_person_months) / (civ_deaths_direct / civ_person_months), with low/best/high built from aligned low/central/high scenario pairs. [corrected: formula was previously inverted (civ_rate/mil_rate); now uses standard mil_rate/civ_rate per definitions.md]
uncertainty_note: Publishable only as a campaign-level, scenario-bounded DR. Because the military denominator is likely conservative relative to the military-plus-police death numerator, the ratio may be biased downward.
